title: Green Chicken Curry
excerpt: Ingredients 15 ml peanut or sunflower oil 65 ml green curry paste 1 bunch finely chopped spring onions 15 ml grated fresh ginger root 10 ml roasted and finely crushed coriander seeds 10 ml roasted and finely crushed cumin seeds 1 ml ground cloves 1 coarsely torn bay leaf grated zest of 2 limes or lemons 15 ml lemon or …
html:
Ingredients

  • 15 ml peanut or sunflower oil
  • 65 ml green curry paste
  • 1 bunch finely chopped spring onions (spring onions)
  • 15 ml grated fresh ginger root
  • 10 ml roasted and finely crushed coriander seeds
  • 10 ml roasted and finely crushed cumin seeds
  • 1 ml ground cloves
  • 1 coarsely torn bay leaf
  • grated zest of 2 limes or lemons
  • 15 ml lemon or lime juice
  • 750 ml coconut milk (or plain cream)
  • 60 ml sugar
  • 7.5 ml salt (or 10 ml fish sauce ‘nam pla’)
  • 600 g chicken breasts, cut into 1 cm strips
  • 250 ml each thinly sliced baby marrow, frozen peas, broccoli florets and Chinese baby spinach leaves (tatsoi)
  • cornflour (Maizena), as needed
  • 125 ml each finely chopped parsley and coriander leaves
  • 65 ml finely chopped mint leaves

Method

Heat the oil and add the green curry paste, spring onions, ginger root, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, cloves and bay leaf, and fry the mixture until lovely and fragrant.

Add the grated zest, juice, coconut milk, sugar and salt and let the mixture cook uncovered on high heat until the sauce has reduced and thickened a little, about ten minutes.

Add the chicken and cook for two minutes, followed by the baby marrow, peas and broccoli. Cook for another three minutes and then add the Chinese spinach leaves.

Now mix enough cornflour with a little water to thicken the mixture to your taste and stir it in. Keep stirring until the sauce is as thick as you like it. Then stir in the parsley, coriander and mint leaves and serve.

Makes 6 servings with jasmine rice and lime or lemon wedges.

Easy!
